chunks.text: Nikola Tesla was born in 1856 in Smiljan, in what is now Croatia.
He later moved to America and, from the late nineteenth to the early twentieth century
became an immigrant inventor who transformed the world of electricity.
His greatest contribution was to make alternating current electricity practical.
At the time, Thomas Edison was promoting direct current.
Tesla argued for the advantage of alternating current for long - distance transmission
and a " war of currents " arose between them.
As a result, the alternating current system became the standard of the world's power grid.
Today
almost all of the electricity that reaches our homes lies on the line that Tesla's vision drew.
Tesla's imagination did not stop there.
He had already pictured the foundation of wireless communication, wireless power transmission, radio
and radar in Edison's age.
However, his life was never happy.
His business partnership did not go well
and his Wardenclyffe Tower project collapsed for lack of funding.
Many of his patent did not bring him enough reward.
Tesla died in 1943, in a hotel room in New York, in poverty.
The recognition of his genius spread widely only after his death.
Today
we drive cars that bear his name and live within part of the future that he had imagined.
